project_structure: pnpm monorepo (fabric-monorepo v2.0.0-rc.9) with three packages: packages/shared (zod schemas, i18n, types, errors), packages/server (MCP tools + HTTP API + services like extract-knowledge, review, doctor, knowledge-meta-builder), packages/cli (commands: init, hooks, uninstall, doctor, scan, plan-context-hint, serve + install/skills-and-hooks.ts that copies templates into client subtrees). Three-mirror skill layout: packages/cli/templates/skills/{fabric-archive,fabric-review,fabric-import}/SKILL.md is the SOURCE OF TRUTH; .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ are DOWNSTREAM COPIES populated by `fab init` (and re-applied by `fab hooks install`); .cursor has no Skills concept. No prebuild/derivation script — the sync is install-time copy via packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts (SKILL_DESTINATIONS const). scripts/ holds standalone gates (rc7-coverage-gate.mjs, lint-protected-tokens.ts, sync-versions.mjs) — there is no scripts/sync-skills.ts. Migration commands convention: new top-level CLI commands live under packages/cli/src/commands/<name>.ts (see uninstall.ts:1, scan.ts) and are wired through commands/index.ts; one-shot migrations on disk have NO prior precedent in this codebase (uninstall is the closest, but it's a stable lifecycle command, not a migration).

relevant_files.rationale: Defines BOTH FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape (L476, used by extract-knowledge MCP tool) AND the FabReview modify schema (L531) where relevance_scope/relevance_paths currently live. Any contract fix that lets fab_extract_knowledge accept scope/paths must add those fields to _FabExtractKnowledgeInputBaseSchema and re-export the .shape.

key_code.description: Base zod object whose .shape is exported as FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape. Currently has recent_paths, type, slug, layer, proposed_reason, session_context — but NO relevance_scope/relevance_paths. Adding them here flows automatically into MCP tool registration via registerTool({ inputSchema: .shape }).
Sibling export of base.shape that registerExtractKnowledge passes to McpServer.registerTool. ZodEffects (the superRefine result) has no .shape, hence the dual export pattern.
Has relevance_scope: z.enum(['narrow','broad']).optional() + relevance_paths: z.array(z.string()).optional() on the changes object. Pattern to copy for the extract-knowledge contract.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: this is the upstream zod source — every test that constructs FabExtractKnowledgeInput, every snapshot in tool-contracts.test.ts, every MCP client integration depends on its exact field surface.

relevant_files.rationale: renderFreshEntry() at L217-263 hard-codes the frontmatter YAML keys (type, maturity, layer, created_at, source_sessions, proposed_reason, tags, x-fabric-idempotency-key). It writes NO relevance_scope / relevance_paths today. Skills currently propose `[scope=narrow|broad]` in their batch review preview but the actual pending entry never persists those fields — the contract gap is here.

key_code.description: Builds pending markdown frontmatter + body. To persist scope/paths the frontmatter array on L223-234 must be extended with `relevance_scope: <value>` and `relevance_paths: [<...>]` lines, matching the YAML flow-style array shape that doctor.ts:628 expects via RELEVANCE_PATHS_LINE_PATTERN.
Service entry point. Needs to thread input.relevance_scope/relevance_paths from the new FabExtractKnowledgeInput shape into renderFreshEntry args.
Internal struct. Must gain relevanceScope + relevancePaths fields.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: this service is the write-side terminus. Its current rendering is what makes the Skill→pending→canonical contract break — Skill assigns scope, body never records it, downstream doctor #23 lint fires on entries the user thought were correctly scoped.

relevant_files.rationale: MCP tool registration site (L6, L24). registerTool consumes FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape as inputSchema. Re-runs through this file are zero-touch IF the .shape export is updated, but the snapshot at packages/server/__tests__/__snapshots__/tool-contracts.test.ts.snap is dependent on this binding and WILL drift.

key_code.description: Calls server.registerTool('fab_extract_knowledge', { inputSchema: FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape, ... }). Re-exports the type from shared. No source changes needed if the schema is extended properly.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: thin re-export — the type binding here is what makes shared schema changes propagate to MCP clients via the tool annotations.

relevant_files.rationale: Golden snapshot test (L73-85): zodToJsonSchema(FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema) → toMatchSnapshot. Adding relevance_scope / relevance_paths to the schema WILL fail this snapshot and require `pnpm test -u` re-baseline. This is the canary for any contract change.

key_code.symbol: tool contracts > fab-extract-knowledge contract matches snapshot
key_code.description: Snapshot test compares full JSON-schema output. Failure prints `[contract drift] Tool contract for fab-extract-knowledge changed. If intentional, run: pnpm test -u`.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: THE primary test signal for contract drift. Every change to FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema funnels through this snapshot.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot artifact produced by tool-contracts.test.ts. Must be re-baselined when the input schema gains new fields. Reviewers should diff this file to confirm only intended additions.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: derived state — confirms exact JSON-Schema shape consumed by MCP clients (Claude Code / Codex CLI / Cursor).

relevant_files.rationale: Unit tests for FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema (L129-213) and FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema (L214+). Currently parses inputs WITHOUT relevance_scope/relevance_paths. Adding those fields needs new positive/negative parse cases (optional+default verification, enum validation for narrow/broad, array-of-string validation for paths).

key_code.description: Six parse/safeParse cases covering existing required + optional fields. Pattern to copy for new relevance fields. Note: test currently exercises the superRefine for source_sessions XOR source_session — adding relevance fields should not interact with that refine.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: the shared-package canary for schema drift; runs against zod directly without MCP plumbing.

relevant_files.rationale: End-to-end service tests (668 lines). buildInput helper at L41-55 constructs FabExtractKnowledgeInput partials — must extend default to cover relevance_scope/relevance_paths. Existing assertions on frontmatter content (e.g. L78-80 `expect(fileContents).toMatch(/^type: decisions$/mu)`) form the template for new relevance-field assertions.

key_code.description: Test helper. Currently sets recent_paths default to []. Needs additional defaults for relevance_scope/relevance_paths so existing tests stay green when fields become optional.
First and most-stable test. Pattern: writes pending, reads back, asserts frontmatter regex matches. New tests should follow this for relevance_scope: narrow / relevance_paths: ["src/**"] persistence.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: highest-volume test file touching the write-side. Test edits here are the single biggest mechanical cost of the schema change.

relevant_files.rationale: Tool-handler integration tests (175 lines). t.handler() invocations at L77-82, L112-117 don't supply scope/paths today — they will continue to work if the new fields are optional, but new tests should verify they propagate through the registerTool boundary into structuredContent / pending file body.

key_code.symbol: invokes extractKnowledge service end-to-end and returns structured content
key_code.description: Validates MCP-handler ↔ service plumbing. Add a sibling test that supplies relevance_scope='narrow' + relevance_paths=['src/auth/**'] and asserts the written pending file contains those frontmatter lines.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: confirms field flows MCP request → service → disk; critical to ensure the new shape isn't dropped at the registerTool boundary.

relevant_files.rationale: Today's relevance_scope/relevance_paths owner: the modify action in FabReviewInputSchema. After contract fix the field stops being review-modify-exclusive — review.ts must NOT downgrade or strip scope set by extract; in particular the team→personal auto-degrade path (emits knowledge_scope_degraded event) must keep working unchanged.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: downstream consumer of the new fields once they're written at extract time. The auto-degrade contract (narrow team→personal ⇒ broad+[]) is invariant across this change.

relevant_files.rationale: 1506-line review test suite. test_review_modify_canonical_entry (L468) and test_review_modify_layer_flip_auto_degrade (L523) assert YAML flow-style frontmatter (`relevance_paths: [src/auth/**, packages/auth/]`) — once extract writes the same shape natively, these tests should still pass (entries are now born with scope, modify still rewrites).

key_code.description: Asserts that modify accepts relevance_scope=narrow + relevance_paths=['src/auth/**','packages/auth/']. Defines the canonical frontmatter format that extract-knowledge.ts must mirror.
Auto-degrade emits knowledge_scope_degraded event. Pattern shows readEventLedger consumer wiring — also a downstream of pending creation.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: defines the YAML frontmatter format (flow-style array, unquoted entries) the new write path must produce verbatim.

relevant_files.rationale: Hosts lints #23 narrow_no_paths (L424+), #24 relevance_paths_dangling, #25 relevance_paths_drift. RELEVANCE_SCOPE_LINE_PATTERN (L627) + RELEVANCE_PATHS_LINE_PATTERN (L628) regex-parse the exact YAML lines extract-knowledge.ts must emit. Lint #23 fires on canonical narrow entries with empty paths — once extract writes the fields directly, fewer entries should false-positive on this lint.

key_code.description: /^relevance_scope:\s*("?)(narrow|broad)\1\s*$/mu — accepts bare or double-quoted enum. extract-knowledge.ts must write bare form to stay consistent with review.ts.
/^relevance_paths:\s*\[([^\]]*)\]\s*$/mu — flow-style array on single line. Entries comma-separated, optionally double-quoted.

Yields {visit, scope, paths, absPath} for every canonical entry. Default scope='broad' when field absent — this is the silent-fallback contract today; new pending entries can opt out by writing the field explicitly.
Pure parsers. Will see new fields automatically once extract writes them.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: doctor is the READ side of the relevance contract — lint #23 is the visible failure mode that the contract fix should reduce/eliminate. Doctor's regex parsers DEFINE the exact YAML shape required.

relevant_files.rationale: 3139-line test suite covering lints #23/#24/#25. Asserts the existing default-broad fallback. New tests needed for: (a) pending entries born with scope=narrow + paths=[...] no longer trigger #23, (b) tests that fed an old pre-fix pending entry (no scope field) still get default-broad behavior.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: hosts the lint-side contract assertions; new fields must coexist with the schema default-broad fallback.

relevant_files.rationale: Builds .fabric/agents.meta.json from canonical entries. Reads relevance_scope / relevance_paths to populate the meta columns (agentsMetaSchema). Consumed by fab_plan_context. Won't need code changes (it already reads the fields), but new entries that carry them at extract time mean the meta is correct from first approve onward.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: downstream meta index consumer; benefits transparently from the fix without code change.

relevant_files.rationale: 781-line test suite for the meta builder. Validates agentsMetaSchema + knowledgeTestIndexSchema parsing. May need a new fixture entry verifying that meta correctly reflects extract-written relevance fields.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: indirect — verifies the round-trip from raw frontmatter through meta JSON.

relevant_files.rationale: Property-based round-trip tests for the API-contract zod schemas. Will need a clause for the new relevance fields if it currently iterates over FabExtractKnowledgeInputSchema (it does — referenced in grep hits L? for relevance_scope/relevance_paths).
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: schema-level round-trip canary — guards against fields that fail zod re-parse.

relevant_files.rationale: ACTUAL three-mirror sync mechanism. SKILL_DESTINATIONS const (L76-89) declares the .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ destinations; the install helpers copy `packages/cli/templates/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` into both. This is the only sync — there is NO scripts/sync-skills.* and NO prebuild hook in root package.json (verified). Editing the source-of-truth SKILL.md is INSUFFICIENT; users must re-run `fab init` (or `fab hooks install`) to refresh their copies.

key_code.description: {fabricArchive: ['.claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md', '.codex/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md'], fabricReview: [...], fabricImport: [...]}. The single source of truth for which client subtrees receive Skill copies. Note: NO .cursor entry (Cursor has no Skills concept).
Template source paths: skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md, fabric-review, fabric-import. Resolved against packages/cli/templates/ at install time.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: solves a critical unknown — there is no derivation script or prebuild hook; the sync only happens via `fab init`. Implication: if the Skill needs new wording about scope/paths after this fix, the in-repo .claude/.codex copies will silently lag until reinstall.

relevant_files.rationale: 472-line integration test for the three-mirror install. Asserts that fab init produces matching .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ contents (L109-142, L378-383). If the source-of-truth SKILL.md changes, this test confirms downstream propagation.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: the only test that exercises the three-mirror sync end-to-end.

relevant_files.rationale: Tests the protected-tokens lint (scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts). Per-skill MCP tokens are: fabric-archive=[fab_extract_knowledge], fabric-import=[fab_extract_knowledge,fab_review], fabric-review=[fab_review]. SKILL.md edits MUST keep these tokens literal — relevance_scope/relevance_paths additions are content, not tokens, so this lint is unaffected by the contract fix but acts as a guard against accidental token loss.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: NOT directly about relevance_scope, but it is the safety net that prevents accidental MCP-tool-name drift while editing skills.

relevant_files.rationale: Standalone lint binary. SKILL_REQUIRED_TOKENS = ['MUST','NEVER','.fabric/knowledge/'] (L38). SKILL_MCP_TOKENS map (L43-47) keyed by skill directory name. PROTECTED_TOKENS registry parse (L80-95). Walks BOTH packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ AND packages/cli/templates/skills/ — sole source of truth, doesn't peek into .claude/.codex copies (which is the right call since those are derived).

key_code.description: Token contracts. Adding new fields to a skill SKILL.md (e.g. `relevance_scope: narrow|broad`) does NOT need a token-list update unless the field name becomes a load-bearing English anchor.

Entry point. Walks bootstrap + skills directories, validates registry. Runs on every CI push via lefthook hook (root package.json L18) — would catch a SKILL.md regression that drops MUST/NEVER/fab_extract_knowledge.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: the lint that protects the three-mirror SOURCE-OF-TRUTH SKILL.md files from token drift. Coexists peacefully with relevance-field additions.

relevant_files.rationale: PROTECTED_TOKENS registry — string anchors that must survive i18n. lint-protected-tokens.ts parses this file at runtime (L15-22). Adding relevance_scope/relevance_paths as registry entries is OPTIONAL — only do so if the field names become load-bearing tokens that i18n must preserve verbatim.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: lint-side registry. Likely no edit needed unless team wants the field names i18n-locked.

relevant_files.rationale: Source of truth for fabric-archive Skill. Phase 1.5 (L214-216) describes `scope=narrow|broad` + relevance_paths derivation. Today the skill PROPOSES these fields in batch review but `fab_extract_knowledge` doesn't accept them — after the fix the skill must pass them through. The skill copy at .claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md AND .codex/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md are dist-only.

key_code.description: Heuristic for narrow vs broad + edit_paths derivation rules. After contract fix this content must remain (it's the LLM heuristic), AND the Phase 2 fab_extract_knowledge call shape must add relevance_scope + relevance_paths arguments.
The fab_extract_knowledge invocation site. Must learn to pass the two new fields.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: source-of-truth SKILL.md — both upstream (Skill drives the contract) and downstream (must update once the contract accepts the fields). .claude and .codex copies are stale until next fab init.

relevant_files.rationale: Source-of-truth for fabric-import Skill (cold-start enrichment). Also calls fab_extract_knowledge per Phase X. Must align scope/paths plumbing if extract input shape changes.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: second producer of pending entries via fab_extract_knowledge; same contract change applies.

relevant_files.rationale: Source-of-truth for fabric-review Skill. Doesn't call fab_extract_knowledge directly (uses fab_review instead) — so this skill is mostly UNAFFECTED by the extract input contract fix; only relevant if the review action receives new relevance-field semantics.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: bystander skill — kept in scope only to ensure review's modify action still reads correctly when entries arrive with scope already set.

relevant_files.rationale: DOWNSTREAM COPY — populated by fab init from packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md. Editing this file directly is anti-pattern; it WILL drift on next `fab init` reinstall. In-repo this copy is committed (visible in git status) — keep it in sync with the source as part of the change.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: confirms the in-repo copies are committed dist artifacts. After source change, MUST regenerate via fab init or hand-mirror.

relevant_files.rationale: DOWNSTREAM COPY for Codex CLI client. Identical content to .claude/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md (verified by install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L129-130). Same regen rule applies.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: parallel dist copy; ensures Codex CLI sees the same Skill behavior.

relevant_files.rationale: fabricConfigSchema. The grep showed 17 files reference it but adding a CLIENT-CONFIG knob for this fix (e.g. opt-out of pending relevance_paths) is NOT REQUIRED — the contract change is purely on the MCP input schema. This file stays unchanged unless we choose to gate the new fields behind a config toggle (recommend: don't).
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: confirms config surface is OUT OF SCOPE for this fix.

relevant_files.rationale: Closest precedent for a top-level CLI command that mutates on-disk state. Three-stage pipeline (scaffold/bootstrap/mcp), --plan dry-run, --yes confirmation, idempotent. If we add a migration command (e.g. `fab migrate-relevance-fields`) it should follow this skeleton: defineCommand, plan/confirm/execute, atomic writes, dev-mode logger.

key_code.description: defineCommand({...}) with args { plan, yes, force, debug, target }; orchestrator dispatches three stage helpers; idempotent + best-effort. Excellent template for a one-shot migration command.

relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: answers the open question 'where does a migration script live?' — packages/cli/src/commands/<name>.ts following the uninstall pattern. scripts/ is for CI/lint gates, not user-facing migrations.

relevant_files.rationale: Command registry. New CLI subcommands (e.g. `fab migrate-relevance-fields`) MUST be wired in here following the existing init/hooks/uninstall/doctor/scan/serve/config/plan-context-hint pattern. Snapshot test cli-surface.test.ts.snap will fail on add and must be updated.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: the registration entry point for any new migration command.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot of `fab --help` output. Adding any new subcommand (e.g. migrate) requires re-baselining this snapshot via `pnpm test -u`.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: catches CLI-surface drift.

relevant_files.rationale: Test patterns for a stateful CLI command (mkdtemp + scenario + atomic-write verification). Template to copy for a migration command's test file.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: testing pattern for a new migration command.

relevant_files.rationale: appendEventLedgerEvent + readEventLedger — used by review.ts to emit knowledge_scope_degraded. If the contract fix adds a new event (e.g. relevance_paths_seeded_at_extract), it would funnel through here. Otherwise no edit.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: event-emission infrastructure shared by all knowledge mutations.

relevant_files.rationale: Event-type zod schemas. knowledge_scope_degraded event lives here. If we add a new event type for migration runs (e.g. `relevance_fields_migrated`), it needs a schema definition here AND a downstream consumer (likely doctor.ts surfacing a count).
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: source of truth for event payload shapes consumed by reviewers + doctor + the Stop hook.

relevant_files.rationale: Append-only event ledger on disk. Today contains knowledge_scope_degraded entries (verified via grep). Consumers: review.ts (writer), doctor.ts (reader for some lints), fabric-hint hook (tail-reader, packages/cli/templates/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs), fab-archive Phase 0/0.0 (tail scanner per SKILL.md L26-43).
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: shared state surface — every consumer (CLI + MCP + skill + hook) reads it; ensures no consumer breaks from new event types.

relevant_files.rationale: Stop hook tail-reads events.jsonl for archive/review/import signals. Doesn't currently consume knowledge_scope_degraded — but it IS the file with the broadest event-consumption surface. New event types should be confirmed not to false-trigger any signal here.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: downstream events.jsonl consumer (hook side).

relevant_files.rationale: ONLY existing pending entry (find -name '*.md' under .fabric/knowledge/pending → count 1). Migration script scope is therefore tiny in THIS repo; in user repos the count may vary. Migration MUST be idempotent across {0, 1, N} pending entries.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: confirms migration target volume is in single digits for this repo — low blast radius.

relevant_files.rationale: Grep confirmed file exists. Hosts user-facing config docs. If we extend fabricConfigSchema (e.g. a relevance_default_scope knob), this is the only doc that needs the new field. Recommendation: skip the config knob; no doc edit required.
relevant_files.topic_relation: dependencies exploration: documentation surface — touched only if fabricConfigSchema gains entries.

patterns: Three-mirror sync = INSTALL-TIME COPY, not prebuild: packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts is the ONLY mechanism that copies templates/skills/<name>/SKILL.md into .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/. No scripts/sync-skills.*, no root package.json prebuild hook (verified). Implication: editing source-of-truth + committing both dist copies is the only honest pattern (otherwise users carry stale copies until next `fab init`). Schema → MCP exposure pattern: shared/schemas/api-contracts.ts defines a base zod object → exports baseSchema.shape as <Name>InputShape → exports baseSchema.superRefine(...) as <Name>InputSchema; the MCP tool registration uses `.shape`, the runtime parsing uses the refined schema; type is z.infer<typeof refined>. This dual export exists because ZodEffects has no .shape — every new MCP input field must be added to the BASE object, not the refined wrapper. Test contract pattern: every MCP input/output schema has (a) shared/test/api-contracts.test.ts zod parse cases, (b) server/__tests__/tool-contracts.test.ts golden snapshot via zodToJsonSchema, (c) services/<name>.test.ts E2E with frontmatter regex assertions. All three need extension for a schema change. Event emission pattern: services mutate disk + call appendEventLedgerEvent({event_type, ...}) with the typed schema from packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts; consumers tail-read via readEventLedger or fs.readFileSync (hooks). YAML frontmatter shape: doctor.ts:627-628 regexes define the wire format — relevance_scope is bare or double-quoted; relevance_paths is single-line flow array `[a, b, c]` with bare or quoted strings. extract-knowledge.ts must produce this shape verbatim to coexist with the doctor lints. CLI command structure: defineCommand + three-stage pipeline + --plan + --yes + atomic writes + dev-mode logger (uninstall.ts is the canonical template; init.ts is the older variant). Migration commands go in packages/cli/src/commands/<name>.ts, not scripts/.
dependencies: EXTERNAL: zod (schema), @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (MCP tool registration), zod-to-json-schema (snapshot), vitest (test runner), citty + @clack/prompts (CLI), minimatch (doctor lint #24 glob matching). INTERNAL hot-path: packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts is imported by 16+ TS files — every test, every MCP tool, every service. Adding fields propagates to all of them via type inference (z.infer). FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape specifically: imported by packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts (registration), packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts (typed param), packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.test.ts (buildInput helper), packages/shared/test/api-contracts.test.ts (parse tests), packages/server/__tests__/tool-contracts.test.ts (zodToJsonSchema snapshot). protected-tokens chain: packages/shared/src/i18n/protected-tokens.ts ← scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts ← packages/cli/__tests__/lint-protected-tokens.test.ts (in-tree integration test that imports from scripts/) ← lefthook pre-commit hook (root package.json `prepare: lefthook install`). Three-mirror chain: packages/cli/templates/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (source-of-truth, lint-checked) → SKILL_DESTINATIONS in skills-and-hooks.ts → install helpers → .claude/skills/ + .codex/skills/ (dist copies, committed in repo, exercised by install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts). Relevance fields chain: shared schema defines them on FabReview modify → review.ts writes them on modify → doctor.ts regex-reads them via iterateRelevanceFrontmatter → lints #23/#24/#25 surface findings → fab doctor CLI surfaces those + knowledge-meta-builder.ts ingests them into agents.meta.json → plan-context.ts uses meta to filter for relevance. After fix, extract-knowledge.ts joins the WRITE side of this chain alongside review.ts.
integration_points: 1. **FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape → fab_extract_knowledge MCP tool** (packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts:24). Adding fields here automatically updates MCP tool annotations seen by Claude Code / Codex CLI / Cursor. Snapshot at packages/server/__tests__/__snapshots__/tool-contracts.test.ts.snap WILL drift; re-baseline with `pnpm test -u`. 2. **Skill→MCP call site**: packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md Phase 2 + fabric-import Phase X — must learn to pass relevance_scope/relevance_paths in the `fab_extract_knowledge` argument map. Each is also committed in .claude/skills/ and .codex/skills/ (re-mirror after edit). 3. **renderFreshEntry frontmatter** (packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts L223-234): YAML keys array must gain two new lines using doctor.ts:627-628 regex-compatible shape (bare enum value + flow-style array). 4. **Doctor lints #23/#24/#25** (packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L424+): consume the new frontmatter automatically once written. Test expectations need adjustment — narrow-with-paths entries born at extract time should NO LONGER trigger #23 narrow_no_paths. 5. **review.ts modify action**: receives relevance_scope/relevance_paths on existing FabReviewInputSchema. Continues to work unchanged but now operates on entries that may already have the fields set. Auto-degrade event (knowledge_scope_degraded) chain remains intact: review.ts emits → events.jsonl → readEventLedger (review.test.ts:499) / fabric-hint hook tail-reader. 6. **knowledge-meta-builder.ts**: reads canonical frontmatter, populates .fabric/agents.meta.json — benefits transparently; no code change needed but verify with a new test fixture. 7. **CLI migration command (proposed)**: lives at packages/cli/src/commands/migrate-relevance-fields.ts (NEW), registered in commands/index.ts, follows uninstall.ts skeleton. Scans .fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md (and possibly canonical .fabric/knowledge/<type>/*.md), detects entries missing relevance_scope/relevance_paths, applies a default policy (likely scope=broad+paths=[] for migration safety — matches schema fallback). Atomic-writes via atomicWriteText from shared/node. Emits a typed event (e.g. relevance_fields_migrated) per touched file. Idempotent: re-runs detect already-migrated entries via field presence and skip. 8. **events.jsonl consumers**: review.ts (writer for knowledge_scope_degraded), fabric-hint.cjs Stop hook (tail-scan for archive/review/import signals — must verify any new event types don't accidentally trigger archive/review/import recommendations), fab-archive SKILL.md Phase 0 (tail-reader for knowledge_proposed anchor), doctor.ts (some lints read events).
constraints: 1. **Backward compat rc.5→rc.7+**: existing pending entries on disk lack relevance_scope/relevance_paths. The new fields MUST be optional in zod and default to undefined in extract-knowledge.ts (omit the YAML lines entirely if absent rather than writing empty defaults — preserves visual diff cleanliness for users who upgrade). doctor.ts already treats missing as broad+[] (L3237-3273), so no behavior change for legacy entries. 2. **Migration idempotency**: any one-shot migration script must be safe to re-run. Detect 'already migrated' by field presence in YAML; skip rather than rewrite. atomicWriteText ensures partial writes don't corrupt entries. 3. **Three-mirror sync invariant**: Every edit to packages/cli/templates/skills/<name>/SKILL.md MUST be propagated to .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and .codex/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (committed dist copies — install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts will catch mismatches in CI). No prebuild script automates this — manual copy or fab init re-run. 4. **Protected tokens preservation**: SKILL.md edits MUST keep MUST/NEVER/.fabric/knowledge/ + per-skill MCP tokens (fab_extract_knowledge for archive+import, fab_review for review). scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts enforces via lefthook pre-commit. 5. **YAML wire format**: scope is bare or double-quoted enum (matches L627); paths is single-line flow array (matches L628). Multi-line block-style YAML breaks both doctor regexes. 6. **Schema dual-export pattern**: NEW fields go on _FabExtractKnowledgeInputBaseSchema (the .object), NOT on the superRefine wrapper — only the base has .shape needed by registerTool. 7. **Snapshot re-baseline discipline**: tool-contracts.test.ts.snap + cli-surface.test.ts.snap drift is EXPECTED for this change; reviewer should diff before accepting `pnpm test -u`. 8. **Personal-root respect**: extract-knowledge.ts writes to either projectRoot/.fabric/knowledge/pending (team) OR FABRIC_HOME/.fabric/knowledge/pending (personal). Migration script MUST follow the same dual-root resolution to avoid clobbering personal layer entries. 9. **Event ledger append-only**: never rewrite events.jsonl; new event types only via appendEventLedgerEvent. 10. **No new fabric-config knob**: avoid extending fabricConfigSchema for this fix — keeps config docs (docs/configuration.md) untouched and avoids per-user opt-in friction.

clarification_needs.question: Migration script: should it touch CANONICAL .fabric/knowledge/<type>/*.md entries (post-approve), or only pending entries in .fabric/knowledge/pending/?
clarification_needs.context: Canonical entries already get scope via fab_review.modify (the existing C3 path); a migration of canonical entries would only fix records that were approved BEFORE C3 shipped and never modified since. Pending entries are a fixed-volume tail (this repo has 1; user repos likely <20). Scope choice drives both blast radius and test coverage.
options: Pending only — minimal, safe, fast (recommended; canonical entries already covered by review modify path)
Pending + canonical — full sweep; needs to respect dual-root (team + personal) and stable_id preservation
Skip migration entirely — let users naturally re-archive or re-modify (highest backward-compat, lowest user-effort spend)

clarification_needs.question: After contract fix, should extract-knowledge.ts WRITE empty default fields (relevance_scope: broad / relevance_paths: []) when caller omits them, or OMIT the YAML lines entirely?
clarification_needs.context: Omitting matches doctor.ts schema-default behavior (treats absence as broad+[]). Writing keeps frontmatter visually self-documenting but produces noisier diffs and conflicts with the existing pending entry's frontmatter style.
options: Omit lines when fields are undefined (recommended — matches doctor default-broad behavior, clean diffs)
Always write defaults (relevance_scope: broad / relevance_paths: []) for self-documentation
Write only relevance_scope (always), omit relevance_paths when empty

clarification_needs.question: Should a new event type (e.g. relevance_fields_migrated) be added to events.jsonl, or is the migration silent?
clarification_needs.context: fabric-hint Stop hook tail-reads events.jsonl for archive/review/import signals. A new event type needs schema + must not false-trigger any hook signal. Silent migration is simpler but invisible to fab doctor and to users reviewing the ledger.
options: Add typed event relevance_fields_migrated (one event per touched file) — auditable, low surprise
Add a single aggregate event relevance_migration_run (count + summary) — quieter ledger
Silent migration — no event; rely on git diff for visibility

_metadata.task_description: fabric 三 skill 合约修复 + 优化方案 — dependencies exploration. 同 integration-points 的 task，需了解 schema 改动后哪些下游测试/lint 会牵连; FabExtractKnowledgeInputShape 引用方、fabricConfigSchema 同步面、protected tokens lint、migration 脚本落位、doctor 中 relevance_scope/paths 相关 lint #23、三镜像同步机制、events.jsonl 消费者面。
